summary: Completed the ftpikachu3.c decompilation slice by moving a large block of Pikachu special-move logic from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s into C, expanding Pikachu attribute layout fields, adding needed prototypes, and updating action-table references in both Pikachu and Pichu asm from local lbl_ symbols to func_ symbols. No review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: Added roughly 397 lines to src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu3.c, replacing most of the corresponding Pikachu assembly block removed from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s.

Implemented many previously-asm Pikachu functions in C, including transition/action callbacks around func_80124EAC, func_80124F24, func_801250FC, func_8012515C, func_80125414, func_80125474, func_801254D4, func_80125528, func_80125774, func_801257D4, func_80125834, func_801258A0, func_80125A54, func_80125CD0, and func_80125D28_file_end.
Replaced temporary extern float labels lbl_804D9910 and lbl_804D9914 in ftpikachu3.c with literal constants such as 0.0f and 1.0f in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls.
Expanded ftPikachuAttributes in ftpikachu.h by replacing padding after x30 with explicit f32 fields x34 through x58, reducing the padding array accordingly.
Changed ftpikachu.h to include melee/ft/ftcommon.h instead of melee/ft/fighter.h, matching the added C usage of common fighter types and fields.
Added prototypes for pika2 helper callbacks func_80124C90 and func_80124D2C and for multiple pika3 functions in ftpikachu.h.
Renamed the public declaration/comment in include/functions.h from func_80125D28 to func_80125D28_file_end, matching the C function name used for the end of the decompiled file section.
Updated Pikachu and Pichu action-table entries from lbl_80124F64-style local labels to func_80124F64-style function symbols, so shared tables point at decompiled C/global functions.
Left some remaining assembly after the decompiled block in ftpikachu.s, including renaming lbl_80125D80 to func_80125D80 and updating its callback references.
smart_moves: The PR did not only add C; it also updated action tables in both ftpikachu.s and ftpichu.s, preventing stale table references to deleted lbl_ symbols after the functions became C/global symbols.
Replacing lbl_804D9910 and lbl_804D9914 externs with 0.0f and 1.0f removed temporary data-label dependencies once the relevant constants moved into source-level expressions.
Expanding ftPikachuAttributes with explicit x34-x58 float fields made later velocity, acceleration, and timing formulas readable and reusable instead of indexing into padding.

The implementation preserved common fighter transition idioms: ground/air conversion helpers, Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC flag constants, ftcmd var resets, and callback assignment through fighter->cb fields.
Renaming the C boundary function to func_80125D28_file_end made the file-end situation explicit rather than pretending it was a normal resolved symbol.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter action callbacks, update all action-state tables that reference the old asm labels, including related clone/variant character files such as Pichu for Pikachu-shared routines.
Do not leave temporary sdata float externs in C if the assembly constants are removed; prefer direct float literals when that matches and keeps the decompiled source self-contained.

Attribute structs should be expanded as soon as C code starts using previously padded offsets; explicit placeholder names like x34, x38, and x58 are still better than hidden padding for matching work.

C implementations of fighter states often need exact callback field assignments, such as x21BC_callback_Accessory4, x21EC_callback, x21F8_callback, and x21C0_callback_OnGiveDamage, to preserve behavior.
State-variable fields such as x2200_ftcmd_var0 and x2340_stateVar1 are important matching anchors for special-move scripts and should be kept visible rather than abstracted away prematurely.
File-boundary symbols may need special temporary names when only part of an asm file is decompiled; document that in prototypes rather than inventing semantic names without evidence.
naming_conventions: Converted action-table targets from lbl_80124F64-style labels to func_80124F64-style global function symbols.
Used address-based placeholder names for unknown functions, consistent with the project style when semantics are not yet confirmed.
Used ftPikachuAttributes with offset-based field names x34 through x58 for newly identified attribute floats.
Used the explicit temporary suffix _file_end in func_80125D28_file_end to signal a boundary-related symbol rather than a fully understood semantic name.
Kept pika2 and pika3 comments in ftpikachu.h to group prototypes by file/region.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved a contiguous assembly block out of asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpikachu.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftPikachu/ftpikachu3.c, shrinking the asm file by over a thousand deleted lines.
Preserved action-state numeric IDs such as 0x157, 0x158, 0x15A, 0x15B, 0x15C, 0x15D, 0x15E, 0x15F, and 0x160 directly in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC calls.
Used explicit flag constants such as 0x200, 0xA, 0xC4C5284, 0xC4C5286, and 0xC4C5086 in action transitions, matching the asm evidence rather than naming them speculatively.

Kept empty callback functions as empty C functions where the asm bodies were just blr, for example func_80124FDC, func_80124FE0, func_801256B4, func_801256B8, func_8012590C, func_8012594C, func_80125950, func_80125954, func_801259D4, func_80125BAC, and func_80125BB0.
Used small unused local arrays such as s32 unused[2] or s32 unused[3] in several functions, a common stack-shape matching technique in this codebase.
Updated remaining asm references to func_80125D80 after changing the symbol from lbl_80125D80, keeping callback loads consistent.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only stated: completed ftpikachu3.c.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for semantic names for the action IDs and flag constants used in ftpikachu3.c.
Search for subsequent cleanup of func_80125D28_file_end to see whether the file-boundary temporary name was resolved.
Compare Pichu action-table references after this PR to ensure all shared Pikachu callback labels were converted consistently.
Look for later ftPikachuAttributes field naming beyond x34-x58 once move parameters were understood.
